behavior_summary: U8P_GRAVEGG::hatch runs as a suspended process that orchestrates an egg-hatching sequence: it freezes the avatar (stasis), fades the screen, teleports the avatar/camera to the egg, removes/creates item representations (egg/creature), fades back in, optionally plays a U8P_GRAVEGG::hatch SFX, runs a short per-frame item animation, flips a completion flag, and restores avatar control.

Two creation branches exist: one creates an item with id 0x02D8 (simple pop) and sets magicWallDone = 1; the other creates id 0x0236 (create-type 0x0E), plays SFX, pops it, runs an animation loop that steps frames via Item::setFrame, and later clears magicWallDone. The branching condition is not explicit in the decompiled nodes.

The process uses multiple external processes/functions (FadeToBlack, teleportToEgg, FadeFromBlack, and a short delay func from spawn->FREE) and multiple suspend points so the sequence occurs across frames rather than instantly.
notable_control_flow: U8P_GRAVEGG::hatch: setAvatarInStasis(0x01) to freeze avatar input/animation.
U8P_GRAVEGG::hatch: call process FadeToBlack and suspend until completion.
U8P_GRAVEGG::hatch: call process teleportToEgg(0x00, 0x0B, 0x06) and suspend.
U8P_GRAVEGG::hatch: conditional branch A — destroy prior item handle (local1), Item::create(type=0x00, id=0x02D8, out=local1) and Item::pop(..., local1); set magicWallDone = 0x01.

U8P_GRAVEGG::hatch: conditional branch B — destroy prior item, Item::create(type=0x0E, id=0x0236, out=local1); playSFX(local1, 0x64, 0x31); Item::pop(..., local1); initialize local2 = 0 and run an animation loop that calls Item::setFrame(frame = 0x0E - local2, local1) and yields via spawn->FREE::func1C19 until local2 > 0x0E; later set magicWallDone = 0x00.
U8P_GRAVEGG::hatch: call process FadeFromBlack and suspend; setAvatarInStasis(0x00) to restore control; end process.
open_questions: What specific runtime condition selects the two Item::create branches (egg type, player state, or global flag)? The condition in the dump is a raw constant and not explicit.
What gameplay meaning does the global flag magicWallDone encode? It is toggled during the sequence but its higher-level purpose is unclear.
Are the numeric item ids (0x02D8 and 0x0236) specific creature/egg types and which in-game assets do they map to?
Is local2 represented in game data as a genuine local counter (as inferred) or was it treated as a flag in the decompiled output?

main_function.name: U8P_GRAVEGG::hatch
main_function.purpose: Run a multi-frame egg-hatching visual/sound sequence: freeze player, transition view, replace or spawn the egg/creature item, animate emergence, update progress flag, and restore player control.
entry_conditions: Invoked as a process (processType 0x0210) — likely started when an egg should U8P_GRAVEGG::hatch or when an item-specific 'U8P_GRAVEGG::hatch' event triggers.
Contains suspend points and process calls (FadeToBlack, teleportToEgg, FadeFromBlack), so it is intended to run over multiple frames rather than inline.

function_map.name: U8P_GRAVEGG::hatch
function_map.role: orchestrates egg-U8P_GRAVEGG::hatch sequence (visuals, item create/destroy, SFX, animation, flags)

function_map.name: setAvatarInStasis
function_map.role: engine call to freeze/unfreeze avatar input/animation

function_map.name: process FadeFromBlack
function_map.role: visual fade-in transition after teleport/item changes

function_map.name: Item::create
function_map.role: create an item instance (two observed id/type variants used as different branches)

function_map.name: Item::pop
function_map.role: spawn/drop the created item into the world using given parameters

function_map.name: Item::destroy
function_map.role: destroy an existing item handle (cleanup before creation)

function_map.name: playSFX
function_map.role: play U8P_GRAVEGG::hatch/emergence sound effect for the created item

function_map.name: Item::setFrame
function_map.role: set displayed animation frame on item during per-frame animation loop

function_map.name: spawn->FREE::func1C19
function_map.role: short per-iteration delay/yield used to pace animation loop

external_calls.purpose: freeze (arg 0x01) and later unfreeze (arg 0x00) the avatar during the U8P_GRAVEGG::hatch sequence
fade to black before teleport/scene change; U8P_GRAVEGG::hatch process suspends until it completes
move avatar/camera to egg location as part of the transition (observed args 0x00,0x0B,0x06); suspended until complete
fade back in after item changes; U8P_GRAVEGG::hatch process suspends until complete
remove a prior temporary item referenced by local1 before creating the new representation
create either an egg or creature item (two observed variants: create-type 0x00 id 0x02D8, or create-type 0x0E id 0x0236); out handle stored to local1
spawn the created item into the world using constants (0x08, 0x4BFF, 0x1E5F) and local1
play a U8P_GRAVEGG::hatch/emergence sound for the created item (observed args local1, 0x64, 0x31)
drive per-frame animation during emergence loop using frame = (0x0E - local2) and local1
short delay/yield used between animation frames to pace the loop (U8P_GRAVEGG::hatch suspends after calling it)

writes.name: magicWallDone
writes.likely_effect: toggled during the U8P_GRAVEGG::hatch sequence (set to 0x01 in one branch and 0x00 in the other) to mark step/progress/state related to the U8P_GRAVEGG::hatch or an associated puzzle/constraint

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_GRAVEGG::hatch runs as a suspended process that orchestrates an egg-hatching sequence: it freezes the avatar (stasis), fades the screen, teleports the avatar/camera to the egg, removes/creates item representations (egg/creature), fades back in, optionally plays a U8P_GRAVEGG::hatch SFX, runs a short per-frame item animation, flips a completion flag, and restores avatar control.
